name: Sauerbraten- with Coke
recipeIngredient: 4 lb Roast beef (preferably sirloin tip, rump, round, or boneless chuck)
recipeInstructions:
1. Two to four days before serving, wipe the meat with a damp cloth and place it in a large plastic bag.
2. In a large bowl, thoroughly combine vinegar, COCA-COLA, water, onions, celery, carrots, peppercorns, cloves, bay leaves, sugar, and salt. Pour over the meat and seal the bag tightly, laying it flat in a 13 x 9-inch pan. Refrigerate, turning the bag each day (or let marinate up to 4 days for a sour sauerbraten).
3. When ready to cook, remove the meat and set aside (saving the marinade). Dry the surface lightly with flour.
4. In a Dutch oven, heat oil or shortening and brown the meat well on all sides.
5. Add 1 cup of the marinade liquid plus some vegetables and bay leaves. Cover tightly and simmer over low heat or in an oven preheated to 350°F for 3 to 4 hours until fork-tender. If needed, add more marinade during cooking to maintain at least a 1/2-inch layer of liquid.
6. Remove the meat and keep warm until ready to slice.
7. Strain the drippings into a large measuring cup, add several ice cubes, and let stand for a few minutes until the fat separates out. Discard the fat.
8. To make the gravy, combine the remaining ingredients in the Dutch oven. Stir and cook over medium heat for about 5 minutes until thickened. Taste and adjust seasonings as needed.
9. Makes 3 cups of gravy. Serves 8.
